Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates chrysin at the 6-position to form baicalein; also hydroxylates apigenin to scutellarein and 7-O-methylchrysin to negletein in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes baicalein/baicalin biosynthesis and loss of function redirects flux toward chrysin glucuronide accumulation in hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome in vitro enzyme assay with HPLC/MS; substrate feeding assays; RNAi silencing in hairy roots followed by LC-MS metabolite profiling; heterologous expression in Arabidopsis with chrysin feeding.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the target product baicalein and thereby supplies precursor for baicalin.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hairy roots; flowers; exogenous chrysin feeding in yeast and Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in hairy roots (FPKM 59.7978) relative to flowers (FPKM 10.7494).
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in hairy roots relative to flowers.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RNAi hairy roots showed a new peak identified as chrysin glucuronide, indicating competition between hydroxylation and glucuronidation. The supplement uses both SbCYP82D1 and SbCYP82D1.1 naming.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces desired 6-hydroxylated flavones and diverts metabolites to chrysin glucuronide.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-defining hydroxylation step; rate-limiting status not established.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCYP82D1.1 expressed in yeast converts chrysin to baicalein in vitro. 2. It also accepts apigenin and 7-O-methylchrysin to form scutellarein and negletein, respectively. 3. RNAi in hairy roots reduces 6-hydroxylated flavones and causes chrysin glucuronide accumulation, supporting its in vivo role in baicalein/baicalin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates chrysin at the 8-position to form norwogonin; can also produce small amounts of baicalein from chrysin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes norwogonin-derived wogonin/wogonoside biosynthesis and affects levels of baicalein, baicalin, wogonin and wogonoside in RNAi hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast microsome in vitro enzyme assay with HPLC/MS; substrate specificity assays; RNAi silencing in hairy roots followed by HPLC; heterologous expression in Arabidopsis with chrysin feeding.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor norwogonin for wogonin/wogonoside and can also contribute directly to baicalein formation in small amounts.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hairy roots; flowers; exogenous chrysin feeding in yeast and Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in hairy roots (FPKM 195.0871) relative to flowers (FPKM 3.3727).
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in hairy roots relative to flowers.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Fails to produce detectable new products from pinocembrin, apigenin or 7-methylchrysin in vitro. The supplement reports small baicalein formation in addition to major norwogonin production.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Imperfect product specificity may generate minor off-branch baicalein; silencing lowers desired downstream flavones.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-defining hydroxylation step; rate-limiting status not established.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCYP82D2 expressed in yeast converts chrysin mainly to norwogonin. 2. It does not accept pinocembrin, apigenin or 7-methylchrysin under the tested conditions, indicating narrow substrate scope. 3. RNAi hairy roots show altered baicalein, baicalin, wogonin and wogonoside levels, supporting an in planta role in the norwogonin/wogonin branch.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methyltransferase-like enzyme tested with chrysin but no new product was formed in yeast, indicating chrysin is not a substrate under the tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Does not convert chrysin in the yeast feeding assay; therefore it is not responsible for direct chrysin modification in this tested step.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vivo yeast feeding assay with chrysin and HPLC product analysis; RNAi construct/primer design noted in supplement.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Likely acts in a downstream modification branch, but direct connection to target end product from chrysin was not demonstrated here.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream modification step not resolved in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The supplement only explicitly demonstrates lack of activity toward chrysin; the true physiological substrate is not shown in the provided content.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Using this gene for direct chrysin conversion would be ineffective based on the provided assay.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbRTO was tested in yeast with chrysin feeding. 2. No new product was observed. 3. Therefore SbRTO cannot use chrysin as a substrate under the assay conditions reported in the supplement.
